entities.name: Information Security Policy
entities.description: A set of rules for securing company data

entities.name: CISO Alex Rivera
entities.description: Author of the Information Security Policy

entities.name: Data Classification
entities.description: A system for categorizing company data based on sensitivity

entities.name: Public
entities.description: Data that can be freely shared externally

entities.name: Confidential
entities.description: Data that requires need-to-know basis with access controls

entities.name: Restricted
entities.description: Data that requires explicit approval and audit logging

entities.name: Access Control
entities.description: A set of rules for controlling system access

entities.name: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
entities.description: A method of verifying user identity

entities.name: Service Accounts
entities.description: Accounts used by systems to access resources

entities.name: SSH Keys
entities.description: Keys used for secure system access

entities.name: DataSync Pro
entities.description: A company that maintains security certifications

entities.name: SOC2 Type II
entities.description: A security certification for service organizations

entities.name: HIPAA
entities.description: A certification for healthcare information security

entities.name: CyberShield Inc.
entities.description: An external firm that conducts penetration testing

entities.name: Security Team
entities.description: A team responsible for ensuring product security

entities.name: VP of Engineering Lisa Park
entities.description: A person who works with the Security Team to ensure product security

relationships.context: The policy was written by Alex Rivera
Data classification includes the public level
Data classification includes the internal level
Data classification includes the confidential level
Data classification includes the restricted level
Access control uses MFA for system access
Service accounts are subject to access control rules
SSH keys are used for secure system access
DataSync Pro maintains SOC2 Type II certification
CyberShield Inc. conducts penetration testing for DataSync Pro
The Security Team works with Lisa Park to ensure product security
